title: IoT Weather Station (OpenWeatherMap)
tips: Use ESP32's second hardware I2C port if you experience conflict issues driving both the OLED and sensor.
ESP32 Wi-Fi routines can block main execution loops. Use FreeRTOS tasks to separate acquisitions from networking.

title: WiFi Multi-Relay Controller
connections: Relay VCC → 5V Aux (+) , Relay GND → 5V Aux (-)
Tie ESP32 GND to Aux GND
tips: Standard relays draw ~70mA each. Running them directly off ESP32 pins will blow the GPIO pins — use optoisolated relay modules.
Configure active-low relays properly in setup to prevent load clicks during resets.

title: Smart Non-Invasive Energy Meter
connections: SCT-013 Current Sensor Output connected to ADS1115 Channel 0 with 22Ω burden resistor and bias network (two 10k resistors and 10uF cap to 3.3V)
tips: Non-invasive CT sensors like SCT-013 output AC current. A DC bias offset of 1.65V (half of 3.3V) is mandatory to read negative waves safely on standard ADCs.
The ADS1115 high-precision ADC delivers 16-bit resolution, ensuring low-signal accuracy for current spikes.

title: MQTT Sensor Dashboard Node
tips: MQTT is extremely lightweight, making it ideal for low-bandwidth cellular/satellite IoT devices.
Ensure you generate a unique MQTT Client ID for each device; duplicate Client IDs cause continuous reconnect loops on the broker.

title: Dynamic AJAX Web Server
connections: LDR output to Analog Input GPIO 34 via 10kΩ divider
tips: Use ESPAsyncWebServer library to enable non-blocking client connections, preventing network overhead from stalling your hardware loop.
Use the ESP32 SPIFFS/LittleFS tool to upload HTML/CSS assets straight to partition flash.

title: ESP32 WiFi Smart Notice Board
connections: P10 inputs (OE, A, B, CLK, LAT) mapped to ESP32 standard SPI pins via level shifter
P10 VCC/GND → 5V 2A supply, ESP32 USB power
tips: P10 LED panels require 5V logic signals. Driving them directly with 3.3V ESP32 outputs can result in ghosting and flickering; use level shifters like the 74HCT245.
Ensure your 5V power supply has enough current headroom since full-panel white text draws peak currents.

title: ESP32-CAM WiFi Video Streamer
connections: ESP32-CAM GPIO0 connected to GND for programming mode
tips: The ESP32-CAM draws up to 350mA spikes during Wi-Fi transmission. Powering it via FTDI adapter 3.3V lines will cause immediate brownout crashes.
Remove the GPIO0-to-GND jumper wire after flash programming to start normal runtime boot.

title: Local OV2640 Face Detection
connections: Connect GPIO0 to GND to flash, remove to boot
tips: Face detection algorithms require PSRAM (Pseudo-static RAM) allocation to process high-resolution color buffers.
Set camera resolution to QVGA (320x240) to limit memory footprint and maximize inference frame rates.

title: Smart IoT Agriculture Station
tips: Capacitive soil sensors do not corrode because the metallic traces are insulated from raw soil contact.
Wrap agricultural wiring connections in watertight heat-shrink tubing to survive field deployments.

title: Ultrasonic Water Tank Monitor
tips: The JSN-SR04T transducer is waterproof and has a minimum blind spot distance of 20cm. Objects closer than 20cm return invalid echoes.
Place the sensor at the center top of the tank lid to avoid wall echo interference.
